reviews.review_description: For some reason I am not allowed to get the weapons to protect my base. I have bought everything else and this game still won’t allow me to buy rocket launchers. They stay black. A game I paid for that I cannot play!   I’m done.

reviews.review_title: Really Fun Tower Defense Mixed Reality
reviews.review_description:
I have been enjoying this game a lot! Graphics are good and the battleground is awesome on the floor of your own home and you can map your furniture that adds strategy and defense tactics. Mixed reality really blends well with this game. 

I wish it had a difficulty setting though. I find the higher levels too tough.  Anyone has starter  build tips?  I highly recommend this game for strategy lovers though!

After several attempts, I made it past level 20 last night.
About gold deposits appearing outside your play space, here's what I did:
1. Go into the Quest headset Settings, Environment setup, Space Setup, Manage Spaces and Clear History.
2. In the room that you are going to play Forticasa, scan the room.
3. Under Environment setup, select Boundary, create new boundary, select Roomscale and Draw your own boundary. Draw your own boundary LARGER than your scanned area.  

I scanned my home office. I then drew the boundary about three feet past my office walls. This step places my entire scanned office area inside my Roomscale boundary. Since you are playing the game in Passthrough, you can see a wall before you walk into it.

Good gaming y'all.

For the Devs, I have a suggestion. When placing an object, add a confirmation (optional setting) step. A few times, I selected an object and my fingered twitched and I dropped the object on the wrong spot - and I lost my base due to single misplacement. Thx.

I was hoping one day to work on a MR tower defence style game and was so amazed that there was one out....BUT

10$ glad i payed just 1$ or so for it and let me explain....

When the game start the mini menu lags super hard, not much tutorial to explain what does what and in game, barely info to understand whats going on.... Now

For some reason the game is barely visible that you dont know where things come from and when you move things are half transparent, cant see the enemy spawn point.... At the end of my round 5 while i was trying to understand whats up in more detail as you are pretty much left alone....

Enemies wouldnt show up... Stuck somewhere... Closed and came back, no start bottom.... Closed and uninstalled it.... Until there is more polishing, this is not worth 10$... Sorry but good work if you are learning to do mr vr game! Keep working on your project! But base price should be 5$

Love the game it's fun keep up the good work keep improving add more weapons missions battles Torrance different type of soldiers that shoot laser guns they throw grenades how about a blowtorch on a back and can you add more upgrades to your tarot guns once you get to the red gun you can't upgrade it no more need more upgrades on weapons

Decent game,right up until your base starts getting destroyed from things outside your play area. Things shooting long range missiles from outside my play area and there doesn't seem any way to stop it. Happened in 3/3 games now. Can't bring myself to try again.

Love the concept but the controls seems really broken. Select to place a turret or my base and then expecting to just point to where I want to place it. However, it seems to only want to place it right underneath where my controller actually is. How can I place turrets and buildings at the resources around the map (room), without me having to keep walking around all over the place?

I was hoping I was just missing something and was hoping there would be a help tool or tutorial somewhere but there is nothing, not even a controls guide.

Other things in the game controls strangely as well, like not being able to scroll through the build screens with the thumb sticks, instead having to grab and scroll using triggers which just isn’t intuitive.

Hoping I have just misunderstood the controls and the developers can reply to this review and help, at which point I will gladly try the game again and update my review as it really is a cool concept.

UPDATE:

Thanks for the reply from devs. I understand your idea of moving around the room to place the buildings and turrets, but that doesn’t make the game very accessible. I was hoping to be able to play it sitting down, which isn’t possible with the current controls. It would be great to have the option to use the controller pointers to place things as an option.

Updated review to 3 stars and would gladly update review further if more accessibility friendly controls are added so the game can be played while sitting stationary.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thanks a lot for your feedback! 😊
We’re really glad you like the concept!
The game is designed to turn your actual room into a battlefield, so moving around your space is part of the core experience. To place turrets or buildings, you need to grab them from the menu—while holding them with your controller, you can move them freely in space. You’ll see a projection on the ground and a buildable area indicator to help you position them. Once you're happy with the spot, just release to place!
As for the build menu: yes, it also uses the grab-to-scroll logic, which fits with the physical interaction design of the game. That said, your suggestion to allow thumbstick scrolling is a great idea—we’ll definitely look into adding that in future updates.
Thanks again for the detailed review—feel free to reach out anytime. We hope to see you back in the game soon!

reviews.review_title: Get this game. It's soooooo good!!!!
reviews.review_description: I've played Iron Guard and Towers & Powers, both of which are torrent defending games. Compared to Forticasa, I say they all have charm and are good games. Forticasa stands out because you can freely place your torrents almost anywhere, and it can be done in mixed reality.  Forticasa might not be the only torrent game on the Meta Quest, but it is one of the most unique that takes a torrent game to the next level and then some. I love how the enemy comes from the ground and air. In the air, the explosions are impressive. There is a lot of freedom building your base almost anywhere you want and a decent amount of torrents to buy as well as a lab and barracks to make soldiers. This game stops just a little short of being a VR real-time strategy game.

reviews.review_title: Mr. Q. Lovin' this little game.
reviews.review_description:
Normally I don't write reviews but in this case I was very pleased with this VR augmented reality game 😀.

I am now on the endless waves stage and it's challenging.

It's great to see the waves coming out of little portals around my room and defending my base against every wave. One mistake and it's game over! 

I wish it had a save game option! 

This game format has great potential, for example... WW1 or WW2 battles or Rome era with barracks and so
 on. If that version / Option was available or a new Augmented reality game was available like this I would buy it immediately.

For 9€ under 10  $ it's a win in my opinion.

Try it 👍

reviews.review_title: Great little game - needs some tweaks
reviews.review_description:
Love the implementation of the game and it makes me smile seeing the attackers walk and fly across my room towards my base! Good variation of enemy types that steadily build up.

However, it feels like there needs to be some tweaks:
Unit prices seem quite high given how little money the base and the Extractor generate. Being able to upgrade the Extractor to generate more money seems like an obvious element that has been missed.

Research prices for new towers seem incredibly high when you can't generate enough money to research AND build that tower.

No pause or quit buttons? If my base is going to be destroyed and I know I can't stop it, do I really have to sit there and watch it be destroyed?

Having some sort of interaction, abilities or powers would be great to help defend your base - look at Iron Guard and the spaceship that you can use to fire at enemies or call in airstrikes.

Each level seems like you need to use specific turrets instead of being able to play freely.

The Sniper tower is called 'The Best Defense' in the Lab rather than Sniper for some reason. Took me a while to figure out where it was!

Really enjoying the game, some quality of life is needed to really make it shine
1. A way to speed up enemies 
2. A way to end your go without waiting for your base to be destroyed 
3. Starting a wave early grants you extra gold
4. Research timers should be deducted for starting wave early 
5. Not played enough to know if you can upgrade non turret buildings, adding a base upgrade with a small turret would be cool
6. Would be really nice to have landmines and maybe even planes and if a turret is in an elevated position gets extra range.
That's all I can think of right now  thanks for bringing us an awsome mixed reality tower defence

The game is really well-made and a lot of fun. It has potential.
I downloaded it yesterday after briefly seeing it on Reddit. To be honest, I’ve been looking for a game just like this.

I only have two minor suggestions for improvement that I haven't seen yet, though I haven’t spent much time in the game so far:

It would be great to access a game menu during rounds or be able to pause the game.
A tutorial explaining the game in detail, including the different modes, towers, etc., would be helpful.
Other than that, fantastic job! I’m wondering why it has so few reviews.

is a true revolution in the world of augmented reality gaming! Every game is different, and the ability to adapt your strategy to the real-world environment makes it incredibly immersive. The turrets, upgrades, and variety of enemy robots always challenge you to think outside the box.

What impresses me the most is how the game keeps evolving. Every update brings new challenges, content, and improvements that make the experience even more exciting. It never gets boring – in fact, every time I play, I find new ways to improve my defenses!

The price is absolutely worth it for everything the game offers: an immersive, ever-expanding experience that’s worth every penny.

If you’re looking for a game that makes you feel part of a world in constant evolution, FortiCasa is for you!

Great game, but the game freezes often where attackers stop moving, so round never ends. Since there are no save points you, or any menu function, when you quit you have to start again. 

Every freeze causes loss of everything, very frustrating on level 10+

Please add save points, that would resolve much of the frustration.

developer_response.body: Hey there! Thanks for the feedback! We’ve tackled that freeze issue head-on, and we’ve implemented a save feature to make sure your progress is safe. No more starting over—now you can pick up right where you left off, smooth and frustration-free! Enjoy the game, and keep the feedback coming! 😊

reviews.review_description: I spent an hour with it my first try and made it to level 28 before my q3 lost power lol. I just wish I could find more info on what all the structures do. I highly recommend this game especially if you like tower defense and the idea of mixed reality. Well worth the $10 in my opinion.

I've been enjoying this game for the past week. It's essentially a simple tower defence game, but because it's in mixed reality it's a lot of fun. I feel like I've completed the game today, because I got to level 53. Previously I'd only got to about level 10. By the time I got to the mid 40s the game was almost unplayable due to how much it was stuttering and dropping frames, but I thought I'd carry on to see if the game crashed... It didn't crash but there was no point carrying on. The reason for this problem is that you keep adding new buildings until the game struggles to process them all.

The developer seems to be adding little updates and tweaks to the game every day at the moment, so I'll play it again in a few weeks. It's still in Early Access so maybe some further upgrades, enemies and optimisations will appear, but I feel like I've played it enough to get my money's worth.
